title: Dorydium liburnicum
taxonomy: Dorydium liburnicum is classified in the family Winteraceae. It belongs to the order Canellales. This plant is native to Australia and New Zealand. The classification of Dorydium liburnicum is further supported by its genetic and morphological characteristics. The species is also known as the New Zealand dorydium.
uses: Dorydium liburnicum has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Its traditional use includes treating digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea.
